3.happening: Place a pointer at the first character.
3.why: It marks one end of the pair to compare.
3.invariant: Everything outside [left, right] has already matched.

4.happening: Place a pointer at the last character.
4.invariant: left and right bound the unchecked middle.

5.happening: While the pointers haven't met, keep comparing.
5.why: Once they cross, every symmetric pair has matched.
5.invariant: left < right means characters remain to check.

6.happening: Is the left character s[{left}] ({s[left]}) non-alphanumeric?
6.why: Spaces, punctuation, and symbols are ignored in the comparison.
6.invariant: Only letters and digits are ever compared.

7.happening: Skip it — advance the left pointer.
7.why: Non-alphanumeric characters don't count toward the palindrome.
7.invariant: left moves toward the next letter or digit.

8.happening: Is the right character s[{right}] ({s[right]}) non-alphanumeric?
8.why: Same skipping rule applies to the right side.
8.invariant: Only letters and digits are ever compared.

9.happening: Skip it — retreat the right pointer.
9.invariant: right moves toward the next letter or digit.

10.happening: Compare s[{left}] ({s[left]}) and s[{right}] ({s[right]}), ignoring case.
10.why: A palindrome reads the same both ways, case-insensitively.
10.invariant: Matching ends keep the palindrome possible.

11.happening: The two ends differ — not a palindrome.
11.why: A single mismatched pair is enough to fail.

12.happening: They matched — move the left pointer inward.
12.why: This pair is confirmed; check the next pair in.
12.invariant: left advances past a matched character.

13.why: Close in on the middle from both sides.
13.invariant: right retreats past a matched character.

14.happening: The pointers met with no mismatch — it's a palindrome.
14.why: Every symmetric pair matched, using no extra memory (O(1)).

init.happening: Placing a pointer at each end.
init.why: We compare inward from both ends.
init.invariant: left and right bound the unchecked middle.

loop.why: The work finishes when the pointers cross.

move.why: Only letters and digits are compared.
move.invariant: The pointers move toward the middle.

check.happening: Comparing the two end characters, ignoring case.
check.why: A palindrome must read the same both ways.

done.happening: A mismatch — the string is not a palindrome.

return.happening: All pairs matched — it's a palindrome.
return.why: Confirmed in one pass with O(1) memory.
